name: glossary
description: Two-column term/definition table with auto-derived alphabetic range pill.
purpose: Use for jargon-heavy decks where the audience needs a reference page. The runtime auto-adds a range pill (e.g. 'A – G') to the heading.

entries.description: Nested bullets: outer li is the term, inner li is the definition.

skeleton:
<!-- _class: glossary -->

## Glossary

- Adjacency
  - The relationship between two slides that share an audience or context.
- Anchor
  - A title, divider, subtopic, or closing slide that orients the audience.
- Cadence
  - The deck's pacing — how much new information per slide.
sample:
<!-- _class: glossary -->

## Glossary

- Consent
  - A freely given, specific, informed agreement to processing — pre-ticked boxes don't count.
- Controller
  - The party that decides why and how personal data is processed, and carries the legal accountability for it.
- DSAR
  - Data Subject Access Request — a person's demand to see, correct, or delete the data held on them, on a 45-day clock under CCPA.
- PII
  - Personal information that identifies a person, now read broadly enough to cover device IDs, cookies, and IP addresses.
- Processor
  - A party that processes data on the controller's instructions — a vendor, not the decision-maker.

whenToUse.title: Jargon-heavy decks
whenToUse.body: When the audience needs a reference page they can flip back to. Acronyms, domain terms, internal names — anything the speaker won't define inline.

whenToUse.title: Five to eight entries per slide
whenToUse.body: The ledger is sized for a short page. For longer glossaries, split alphabetically across multiple slides — the runtime stamps each with its range pill.

whenToUse.title: Short definitions
whenToUse.body: Each definition is one sentence — a working gloss, not an essay. Long definitions belong on their own subtopic slide where the term is the heading.

antiPatterns.title: Multi-sentence definitions
antiPatterns.body: Each entry is one short line. If a definition needs context or examples, the term deserves its own slide — use subtopic with the term as the heading.

antiPatterns.title: Mixed term lengths
antiPatterns.body: If some terms are single words and others are full phrases, the left column gets ragged. Trim long terms to their canonical short form.

antiPatterns.title: Hand-written range pill
antiPatterns.body: The runtime derives the range pill (e.g. "A – G") from the entries. Authoring it into the heading double-stamps it.

related.name: list-tabular
related.when: rows are key/value reference, not term/definition

related.name: subtopic
related.when: one term needs a full slide of explanation

related.name: actors
related.when: the left column is a named person, not a term

related.name: principles
related.when: the entries are stated rules, not defined terms
